question_text: Let $Y _ { 1 } < \cdots < Y _ { n }$ be order statistics from a continuous distribution symmetric about its mean $\mu .$ Find the smallest $n$ (in integer) such that $P ( Y _ { 1 } < \mu < Y _ { n } ) \geq 0 . 9 9$
solution:
By symmetry and continuity, $P ( X \leq \mu ) = P ( X \geq \mu ) = { \frac { 1 } { 2 } }$ and ties have probability 0. Thus

 $ \begin{array} { r } { P ( Y _ { 1 } < \mu < Y _ { n } ) = 1 - P ( \mathrm { a l l } \ \le \mu ) - P ( \mathrm { a l l } \ \ge \mu ) = 1 - 2 \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right) ^ { n } = 1 - 2 ^ { 1 - n } . } \end{array} $ 

Require $1 - 2 ^ { 1 - n } \geq 0 . 9 9 \Rightarrow 2 ^ { 1 - n } \leq 0 . 0 1 \Rightarrow 2 ^ { n - 1 } \geq 1 0 0$ . Hence $n - 1 \geq \log _ { 2 } { 1 0 0 } \approx 6 . 6 4 3 8$ so the smallest integer is ${ \bigg \lceil } 8 { \bigg \rceil } .$
Quick Tip

For symmetric continuous distributions, probabilities involving $\mu$ often reduce to powers of $\begin{array} { l } { { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } } \end{array}$ since observations fall on either side independently with probability $\begin{array} { l } { { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } } \end{array}$ each.
